pattern: A sorted array puts every negative value before every positive value, so the two counts can be read off from a pair of boundary crossings instead of a full pass.
hints: The array is already sorted, so all the negatives sit together at the front, any zeros sit in the middle, and all the positives sit together at the back.
You only need two boundary positions: where the run of negatives ends, and where the run of positives begins. Everything in between is zero and counts toward neither.

Binary search for the first index that is >= 0 (that index is the negative count) and the first index that is > 0 (subtract that from the length to get the positive count), then return the larger of the two.

edge_inputs.description: all negative values, no positives or zeros
all positive values, no negatives or zeros
entirely zero, neither count should win
a block of zeros sits between the negatives and positives, and the two nonzero counts are equal
far more zeros than nonzero entries, with negatives outnumbering positives
values at the extreme ends of the allowed range

stress.description: large randomly generated sorted arrays spanning the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    nums = sorted(rng.randint(-2000, 2000) for _ in range(n))
    return [nums]
